MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jan-22-2026 Granted Per Written Order

Brian Brumfield, Esq., QUILL & ARROW, LLP Telephone: (310) 933-4271 bbrumfield@quillarrowlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiffs, XINQIANG TAN ANNEY L. TAN SUPERIOR for Plaintiff.

Joe M. Yelda, ESQ., Squire Patton Boggs (US) LLP 213.624.2500 joe.yelda@squirepb.com

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC., ANDERSON ENTERPRISES, INC. D/B/A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. After argument, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2025 Set For Trial On Jan-11-27 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections - Requests For Admission; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Joe M. Yelda
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'s Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections - Requests For Admission.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion for relief from waiver of objections pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 2033.280, subdivision (a), is granted. The court finds that Volkswagen has served a response that is in substantial compliance with Sections 2033.210, 2033.220, and 2033.230; and Volkswagen's failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Nothing in this order precludes Plaintiffs from moving for further responses after meet and confer efforts.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections - Formn Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Joe M. Yelda
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'s Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections - Form Interrogatories.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion for relief from waiver of objections pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 2030.290, subdivision (a), is granted. The court finds that Volkswagen has served a response that is in substantial compliance with Sections 2030.210, 2030.220, 2030.230, and 2030.240; and Volkswagen's failure to serve a timely response was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Nothing in this order precludes Plaintiffs from moving for further responses after meet and confer efforts.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.
